ddls:
CREATE TABLE "errors" (
  "error_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each error */,
  "module_id" INTEGER /* ID of the Azure module */,
  "cmdlet_id" INTEGER /* ID of the cmdlet that triggered the error */,
  "example" INTEGER /* Example number associated with the error */,
  "line" INTEGER /* Line number where the error occurred */,
  "rule_name" TEXT /* Name of the rule that triggered the error */,
  "problem_id" INTEGER /* ID of the problem associated with the error */,
  "severity" INTEGER /* Severity level of the error (1-5) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the error */,
  "extent" TEXT /* Extent of the error (e.g., None, ?????Name='SecurityType') */,
  "remediation" TEXT /* Remediation steps for the error */,
  "error_timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp when the error occurred */,
  "error_status" TEXT /* Status of the error (e.g., Open, In Progress, Resolved) */,
  "assigned_to" INTEGER /* ID of the user assigned to the error */,
  "resolution_time" INTEGER /* Time taken to resolve the error */,
  "repro_steps" TEXT /* Steps to reproduce the error */,
  "environment" TEXT /* Environment where the error occurred (e.g., Development, Staging, Production) */,
  "impact" TEXT /* Impact of the error (e.g., Low, Medium, High) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("error_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_errors_cmdlet_id FOREIGN KEY ("cmdlet_id") REFERENCES cmdlets ("cmdlet_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_errors_assigned_to FOREIGN KEY ("assigned_to") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_errors_module_id FOREIGN KEY ("module_id") REFERENCES modules ("module_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "modules" (
  "module_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each module */,
  "module_name" TEXT /* Name of the Azure module */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the module */,
  "version" TEXT /* Version of the module */,
  "last_updated" TEXT /* Last time the module was updated */,
  "owner" TEXT /* Owner of the module */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("module_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "cmdlets" (
  "cmdlet_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each cmdlet */,
  "cmdlet_name" TEXT /* Name of the cmdlet */,
  "module_id" INTEGER /* ID of the module the cmdlet belongs to */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the cmdlet */,
  "example_count" INTEGER /* Number of examples associated with the cmdlet */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("cmdlet_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_cmdlets_module_id FOREIGN KEY ("module_id") REFERENCES modules ("module_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the user was created */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_access" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access record */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user */,
  "module_id" INTEGER /* ID of the module */,
  "access_level" TEXT /* Access level (e.g., Read, Write, Admin) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_access_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_access_module_id FOREIGN KEY ("module_id") REFERENCES modules ("module_id")
)
